message:
feat: word-level timestamps, cancellation token threading, and conformance hardening

- backend/insanely_fast_native: add word-level timestamp support with
  WordSegment struct and build_segments_and_chunks dispatcher; when
  TimestampLevel::Word is requested, segments are split into per-word
  entries with evenly distributed timestamps and chunks gain a words array
- backend/mod: thread CancellationToken through Engine::run and
  StreamingEngine::run_streaming trait signatures (was hardcoded None);
  add word_timestamps and split_on_word fields to WhisperCppPilot
- backend/whisper_cpp_native: add WordTimestampMode enum and full
  word timestamp control pipeline (explode_segments_to_words,
  group_word_segments_by_len); add no_timestamps flag to
  to_transcription_segments
- cli: clamp speculative_overlap_ms to window_size_ms - 1 to prevent
  overlap >= window
- conformance: fix segment validation bug where end_sec: None reset
  previous_end, bypassing overlap check; trim whitespace in text
  comparison; add five new invariant tests
- orchestrator: make CancellationToken pub; add output format export via
  export::write_artifacts; fix adaptive prediction recording to run after
  awaiting backend result
- process: increase recv_pipe_output timeout from 50ms to 200ms; switch
  to streaming pipe reads
- storage: push sorting and limiting into SQL (ORDER BY/LIMIT) instead
  of in-memory sort
- sync: wrap export files in BufWriter; validate export_format_version
  major version on import; extend validate_sync to compare 5 additional
  fields per run record
- tty_audio: revert protocol version to MIN_PROTOCOL_VERSION; separate
  SessionClose frame handling with duplicate detection and
  validate_session_close
- audio: fix symlink handling in find_file_named (skip instead of match)
- lib: add pub mod export
- main: add validate_sync call after sync import with JSON output
- Cargo.lock: bump ftui crates 0.3.0 → 0.3.1
- docs/engine_compatibility_spec: clarify length mismatch and None
  timestamp comparison semantics
- tests: update conformance harness for policy/invariant fields; remove
  hard per-pair tolerance assertion; add compliance_report generation
- .beads: compact old history snapshots (March 2026) into current tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-20g","title":"Robot Mode: Agent-First NDJSON Interface Enhancement","description":"EPIC: Robot Mode Enhancement

Robot mode is the agent-first interface — line-oriented NDJSON events streamed in real-time. This is how other programs (AI agents, automation scripts, CI/CD) interact with franken_whisper. It MUST be:
- Stable schema (versioned, backward-compatible)
- Deterministic where possible
- Explicit error codes (enumerated, documented)
- Line-oriented (jsonl/ndjson)
- Easy to pipe into other tools

Current implementation is functional but can be enhanced:
1. Schema versioning: Include schema_version in every event so consumers can adapt
2. Error code enumeration: Replace ad-hoc error strings with structured codes
3. Progress estimation: Include estimated_progress_pct in stage events
4. Streaming partial transcripts: Emit partial transcript text as segments are produced (not just at completion)
5. Backend availability query: `robot backends` command to list available backends with capabilities
6. Run comparison: `robot diff <run1> <run2>` to compare transcription results
7. Health check: `robot health` to verify system readiness
8. Input validation: `robot validate --input <file>` to check input before committing to a run

The event schema should be documented as a JSON Schema artifact that can be consumed by typed language generators.

Key files: src/robot.rs (112 lines), src/main.rs (robot command handling)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Robot mode outputs stable, versioned NDJSON schema
- All events have explicit error codes
- Schema versioning supports backward compatibility
- Event ordering is guaranteed monotonic
- All robot mode contract tests pass.","acceptance_criteria":"Robot mode outputs stable, versioned NDJSON schema. All events have explicit error codes. Schema versioning supports backward compatibility. Event ordering is guaranteed monotonic. All robot mode contract tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:08:47.305701227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T20:46:02.725333019Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T20:46:02.725313763Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-ergonomics","epic","phase-2","robot-mode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20g","depends_on_id":"bd-1rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20g.1","title":"Implement robot event schema versioning","description":"Robot mode NDJSON output must be stable. Add schema versioning:

1. Every event includes schema_version: "1.0"
2. robot schema command emits full JSON Schema document
3. Version bump rules: Minor for new optional fields, Major for breaking changes
4. Schema as cargo-embedded resource

Events to version: run_start, stage, run_complete, run_error, run_cancelled (new), partial_transcript (new), backend_probe (new).

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_schema_version_present: Every emit_robot_* function includes schema_version field
- test_schema_valid_json_schema: robot schema output is valid JSON Schema (parse with serde)
- test_all_events_match_schema: Generate each event type, validate against schema
- test_schema_backward_compat: New schema accepts old events (additive only)
- test_schema_command_output: cargo run -- robot schema → valid JSON on stdout
- test_schema_embedded_resource: Schema accessible at runtime without filesystem

LOGGING:
- "Emitting robot event: {event_type}, schema_version={ver}" at TRACE

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every robot event includes a schema_version field
- Version follows semver (major.minor)
- Breaking changes increment major version
- Consumers can check version before parsing
- Unit test: all event types include schema_version
- Unit test: version string parses as valid semver.","acceptance_criteria":"Every robot event includes a schema_version field. Version follows semver (major.minor). Breaking changes increment major version. Consumers can check version before parsing. Unit test: all event types include schema_version. Unit test: version string parses as valid semver.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:24:29.593544023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T17:42:30.520259632Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T17:42:30.520179723Z","closed_by_session":"2026-02-22T12:42:30-05:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["robot-mode","schema","versioning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20g.1","depends_on_id":"bd-20g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20g.2","title":"Enumerate explicit error codes for robot mode","description":"Replace ad-hoc error strings with structured, enumerated error codes.

Scheme: E1xxx (input), E2xxx (backend), E3xxx (storage), E4xxx (acceleration), E5xxx (sync), E6xxx (pipeline), E9xxx (internal).

Each code has: numeric code (stable, never reused), short name, human message, suggested recovery.

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_all_error_codes_unique: No duplicate numeric codes
- test_all_error_codes_have_name: Every code has a non-empty short name
- test_error_code_mapping: FwError variants map to correct error code ranges
- test_error_event_format: {"event": "run_error", "code": "E2001", "name": "backend.unavailable", ...}
- test_recovery_suggestions_present: Every error code has a non-empty recovery suggestion
- test_error_codes_stable: Error code list matches golden file (prevents accidental changes)

LOGGING:
- "Error mapped to code {code}/{name}: {message}" at ERROR per error occurrence

## Acceptance Criteria
- Error code enum covers all failure modes: backend_unavailable, input_not_found, ffmpeg_failed, normalization_failed, backend_error, storage_error, cancelled, timeout, unknown
- Each error code has human-readable message and machine-parseable code string
- Unit test: every FwError variant maps to a unique error code
- Unit test: error codes are stable (no renaming without major version bump).","acceptance_criteria":"Error code enum covers all failure modes: backend_unavailable, input_not_found, ffmpeg_failed, normalization_failed, backend_error, storage_error, cancelled, timeout, unknown. Each error code has human-readable message and machine-parseable code string. Unit test: every FwError variant maps to a unique error code. Unit test: error codes are stable (no renaming without major version bump).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:24:35.693499949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T17:54:58.738638457Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T17:54:58.738570760Z","closed_by_session":"2026-02-22T12:54:58-05:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-ergonomics","error-codes","robot-mode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20g.2","depends_on_id":"bd-20g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20g.2","depends_on_id":"bd-20g.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3i1.3","title":"Add diagnostic PRAGMAs for storage observability","description":"frankensqlite supports diagnostic PRAGMAs that expose internal state. Add observability endpoints:

1. Transaction stats: Active transactions, commits/rollbacks per second
2. WAL state: Current WAL size, checkpoint status, RaptorQ repair symbol count
3. Page cache: Hit/miss ratios, dirty page count
4. MVCC state: Active snapshots, version chain depth
5. Storage size: DB file size, WAL size, total pages

Expose these via:
- Robot mode: `robot storage-stats` command emitting NDJSON
- TUI: Status line showing active transactions, DB size
- CLI: `storage-info` command for debugging

This enables monitoring and debugging of storage behavior in production.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Health check query returns DB size, WAL size, table row counts
- Integrity check (PRAGMA integrity_check) exposed via API
- Stale lock detection with configurable timeout
- Diagnostic output is structured (JSON for robot mode)
- Unit test: health check returns valid metrics on test DB.","acceptance_criteria":"- Health check query returns DB size, WAL size, table row counts. - Integrity check (PRAGMA integrity_check) exposed via API. - Stale lock detection with configurable timeout. - Diagnostic output is structured (JSON for robot mode). - Unit test: health check returns valid metrics on test DB.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:13:36.754448740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T21:19:59.816158762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T21:19:59.816140608Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","frankensqlite","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3i1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3i1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3i1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3i1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3i1.4","title":"Implement schema versioning with migration support","description":"Schema evolution for the three-table DB (runs, segments, events).

Migration framework:
1. _meta table: key=schema_version, value=N
2. On open: check version, run forward migrations
3. Each migration in a transaction
4. Never drop columns (only add)

Planned migrations: v2 (acceleration_json), v3 (speaker_embeddings), v4 (vad_segments), v5 (aligned_words).

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_fresh_db_at_latest_version: New DB has current schema version
- test_migration_v1_to_v2: Open v1 DB → migrate → verify new columns exist
- test_migration_idempotent: Run migration twice → no error
- test_migration_preserves_data: Populate v1 DB → migrate → all data intact
- test_migration_transactional: Inject error mid-migration → ROLLBACK, DB unchanged
- test_version_mismatch_detection: Open DB with future version → clear error
- test_meta_table_created: _meta table exists with correct schema_version
- test_jsonl_includes_version: JSONL export manifest includes schema version

LOGGING:
- "DB schema version: current={cur}, expected={exp}" at INFO on open
- "Migrating schema: v{from} → v{to}" at INFO per migration
- "Migration v{N} complete: {changes_summary}" at INFO

## Acceptance Criteria
- RaptorQ encoding applied to critical DB pages
- Recovery from simulated corruption restores data without loss
- Durability test: corrupt N random bytes, verify recovery succeeds
- Performance: encoding overhead under 5% for normal operations
- Fallback: graceful degradation if RaptorQ unavailable.","acceptance_criteria":"- RaptorQ encoding applied to critical DB pages. - Recovery from simulated corruption restores data without loss. - Durability test: corrupt N random bytes, verify recovery succeeds. - Performance: encoding overhead under 5% for normal operations. - Fallback: graceful degradation if RaptorQ unavailable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:13:36.835763506Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T19:22:26.214967861Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T19:22:26.214926404Z","close_reason":"Schema versioning with _meta table, SCHEMA_VERSION=2, forward-only migrations, DELETE+INSERT for fsqlite INSERT OR REPLACE bug. 6 tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensqlite","migration","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3i1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3i1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3i1.5","title":"Add index optimization for common query patterns","description":"Optimize query performance with targeted indexes.

Current queries:
- list_recent_runs: ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT n → needs INDEX on runs(started_at)
- load_run_details: WHERE id = ? → already has PRIMARY KEY
- segments by run: WHERE run_id = ? ORDER BY idx → needs INDEX on segments(run_id, idx) (already PRIMARY KEY)
- events by run: WHERE run_id = ? ORDER BY seq → needs INDEX on events(run_id, seq) (already PRIMARY KEY)

New indexes needed for planned features:
- Search transcript: CREATE INDEX idx_runs_transcript ON runs(transcript) — but TEXT search needs FTS
- Filter by backend: CREATE INDEX idx_runs_backend ON runs(backend)
- Filter by time range: Already covered by started_at index
- Speaker search in segments: CREATE INDEX idx_segments_speaker ON segments(speaker)
- Confidence filtering: CREATE INDEX idx_segments_confidence ON segments(confidence)

Consider frankensqlite's FTS5 extension for full-text search over transcripts.

Performance targets:
- list_recent_runs(100): < 1ms
- load_run_details: < 5ms (even with 1000+ segments)
- transcript search: < 50ms (with FTS5)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Indexes exist on: runs(created_at), runs(backend), segments(run_id), events(run_id, seq)
- Query performance: list_runs with limit 100 under 10ms on 10K rows
- Query performance: get_segments_for_run under 5ms
- Index creation is idempotent (safe to re-run)

 {"id":"bd-3pf","title":"Testing Infrastructure: Comprehensive Quality Gates","description":"EPIC: Testing Infrastructure

Testing is a first-class concern. The AGENTS.md mandates:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test

Each module needs:
- Unit tests: happy path, edge cases, error handling
- Integration tests: file-based transcription, robot mode JSON contracts, SQLite+JSONL sync/recovery, ffmpeg behavior
- E2E tests: full pipeline from audio file to persisted result

Special testing requirements:
1. Cancellation testing: Use asupersync lab runtime to inject cancellation at every pipeline stage, verify no resource leaks
2. Robot mode contract tests: Validate NDJSON output against JSON Schema
3. Backend mock tests: Test pipeline without real backend binaries (mock subprocess output)
4. TUI snapshot tests: Render TUI to buffer, compare against golden snapshots
5. Performance benchmarks: Track regression in processing time, memory usage
6. Sync roundtrip tests: Export → import → compare, verify no data loss
7. Concurrent persistence tests: Multiple runs writing simultaneously via MVCC
8. TTY audio codec roundtrip: Encode → decode → compare, verify lossless (within codec tolerance)

Testing tools: cargo test, criterion (benchmarks), proptest (property testing), insta (snapshot testing)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test infrastructure supports: mock backends, golden file comparison, E2E pipeline tests, structured logging verification
- All modules have comprehensive unit tests
- E2E test harness exercises full transcription flow
- Performance benchmarks establish baselines
- All tests pass in CI (no external dependencies required).","acceptance_criteria":"Test infrastructure supports: mock backends, golden file comparison, E2E pipeline tests, structured logging verification. All modules have comprehensive unit tests. E2E test harness exercises full transcription flow. Performance benchmarks establish baselines.  {"id":"bd-efr.4","title":"Implement evidence ledger system for all adaptive decisions","description":"ALIEN-ARTIFACT: Evidence Ledger

Every adaptive decision produces an evidence ledger artifact for auditability.

Schema: controller, decision_id (UUID), timestamp (RFC3339), state (JSON), actions_considered (vec of {action, expected_loss, confidence}), action_selected, posterior, loss_estimate, calibration_metric, fallback_triggered, fallback_reason, outcome (post-hoc).

Storage: events table stage=evidence_ledger, also in JSONL export.
Query: robot evidence --run <id>, CLI evidence --last 10, TUI evidence pane.

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_ledger_schema_complete: Every ledger has ALL required fields populated
- test_ledger_uuid_unique: Each decision gets a unique ID
- test_ledger_timestamps_valid: All timestamps are valid RFC3339
- test_ledger_round_trip: Create → persist → load → compare (no data loss)
- test_ledger_query_by_run: Load all ledgers for a specific run_id
- test_ledger_query_recent: Load last N ledgers across all runs
- test_ledger_in_jsonl_export: Evidence ledgers included in JSONL export
- test_outcome_backfill: After execution, outcome field is populated

LOGGING:
- "Evidence ledger created: controller={ctrl}, decision={id}" at DEBUG
- "Evidence ledger outcome: {id} → {outcome_summary}" at DEBUG

## Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence ledger persists: every routing decision, shadow mode results, calibration updates, backend performance metrics
- Ledger stored in frankensqlite alongside run data
- Ledger queryable for trend analysis (which backend is improving/degrading)
- Unit test: routing decision persisted and retrievable
- Unit test: ledger grows monotonically (append-only)
- Export: ledger included in JSONL sync exports.","acceptance_criteria":"Evidence ledger persists: every routing decision, shadow mode results, calibration updates, backend performance metrics.  {"id":"bd-qla","title":"Pipeline Enhancement: Advanced Multi-Stage ASR Processing","description":"EPIC: Pipeline Enhancement

The current pipeline has 5 stages: ingest → normalize → backend → accelerate → persist. This is a good start but misses several powerful capabilities from the legacy systems.

New stages to add (inspired by whisper-diarization's multi-stage approach):
1. VAD Pre-filtering: Voice Activity Detection to identify speech segments before sending to ASR. Dramatically reduces processing time for audio with lots of silence/noise. Inspired by whisper.cpp's Silero-VAD integration.
2. Source Separation: Isolate vocals from background noise/music (Demucs-inspired). Improves speaker embedding accuracy and ASR quality on noisy audio.
3. Forced Alignment: CTC-based timestamp correction after initial ASR. Whisper's timestamps can drift at segment boundaries; forced alignment uses emission probabilities to find true boundaries. Critical for accurate word-level speaker mapping.
4. Speaker Diarization: TitaNet-inspired speaker embedding + clustering. Identify who said what. Currently delegated entirely to backends; bringing this in-house gives more control.
5. Punctuation Restoration: Multilingual punctuation model (12+ languages). The diarization pipeline strips punctuation during alignment; restoration makes transcripts readable.

Pipeline should also support:
- Skip/include stages based on request (e.g., skip diarization if not needed)
- Stage-level caching (don't re-normalize if already WAV)
- Parallel stage execution where possible (e.g., VAD + source separation)
- Progress reporting per stage (for robot mode and TUI)

Each new stage must follow the existing EventLog pattern — emit stage start/complete events with payloads for observability.

Key architectural decision: New stages should be composable and optional, not monolithic. The pipeline configuration should be declarative.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pipeline includes all stages: ingest, normalize, VAD, source separation, backend, alignment, diarization, punctuation, accelerate, persist
- Stages are composable and independently testable
- Each stage emits robot events
- Pipeline handles errors at any stage with proper cleanup
- All pipeline tests pass.","acceptance_criteria":"Pipeline includes all stages: ingest, normalize, VAD, source separation, backend, alignment, diarization, punctuation, accelerate, persist. Stages are composable and independently testable. Each stage emits robot events. Pipeline handles errors at any stage with proper cleanup. All pipeline tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:08:47.223292539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T21:24:27.767745727Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T21:24:27.767721011Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","phase-3","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qla","depends_on_id":"bd-1rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qla","depends_on_id":"bd-38c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-qlt.8","title":"Implement CorrectionEvidenceLedger with per-window correction tracking","description":"PHASE 3 ADAPTIVE INTELLIGENCE -- Persistent evidence ledger that records every speculation decision for analysis, debugging, and adaptive improvement.

FILE: src/speculation.rs (extend)

WHY:
The existing RoutingEvidenceLedger (backend/mod.rs) tracks backend selection decisions. The CorrectionEvidenceLedger is its speculation analog: it records every window's fast/quality comparison, correction decision, and drift metrics. This serves three purposes:
1. Post-hoc analysis: "Why did the pipeline correct window 47?"
2. Adaptive control: The SpeculationWindowController reads this to tune window size
3. Debugging: Full replay of every decision for deterministic reproduction

RELATIONSHIP TO EXISTING EVIDENCE INFRASTRUCTURE:
- RoutingEvidenceLedger (backend/mod.rs:229-381) -- tracks backend selection, capacity 200
- PipelineCx::record_evidence() (orchestrator.rs) -- accepts Value entries
- EvidenceLedgerBuilder (accelerate.rs) -- builds acceleration evidence
- This new CorrectionEvidenceLedger follows the same pattern: structured entries, bounded capacity, JSON export

TYPES:

pub struct CorrectionEvidenceLedger {
    entries: VecDeque<CorrectionEvidenceEntry>,
    capacity: usize,
    total_recorded: u64,
}

pub struct CorrectionEvidenceEntry {
    pub entry_id: u64,
    pub window_id: u64,
    pub run_id: String,
    pub timestamp_rfc3339: String,
    
    // Fast model info
    pub fast_model_id: String,
    pub fast_latency_ms: u64,
    pub fast_confidence_mean: f64,
    pub fast_segment_count: usize,
    
    // Quality model info
    pub quality_model_id: String,
    pub quality_latency_ms: u64,
    pub quality_confidence_mean: f64,
    pub quality_segment_count: usize,
    
    // Drift metrics
    pub drift: CorrectionDrift,
    
    // Decision
    pub decision: String,          // "confirmed" or "corrected"
    pub tolerance_snapshot: CorrectionTolerance,
    
    // Controller state at decision time
    pub window_size_ms: u64,
    pub correction_rate_at_decision: f64,
    pub controller_action: String, // "shrink", "hold", "grow"
    pub controller_confidence: f64,
    pub fallback_active: bool,
    pub fallback_reason: Option<String>,
}

impl CorrectionEvidenceLedger {
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self
    pub fn record(&mut self, entry: CorrectionEvidenceEntry)
    pub fn total_recorded(&self) -> u64
    pub fn entries(&self) -> &VecDeque<CorrectionEvidenceEntry>
    pub fn to_evidence_json(&self) -> Value  // For PipelineCx::record_evidence()
    pub fn diagnostics(&self) -> Value       // Summary stats as JSON
    
    // Analysis methods
    pub fn correction_rate(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_fast_latency(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_quality_latency(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_wer(&self) -> f64
    pub fn latency_savings_pct(&self) -> f64  // How much faster user sees results vs quality-only
    
    // Window size trend
    pub fn window_size_trend(&self) -> Vec<(u64, u64)>  // (window_id, window_size_ms) pairs
}

INTEGRATION WITH PipelineCx:
At the end of a speculative run, the pipeline calls:
  pcx.record_evidence(&correction_ledger.to_evidence_json());
This makes the correction evidence part of the run's permanent evidence trail, alongside routing evidence and acceleration evidence.

INTEGRATION WITH STORAGE:
The evidence entries should be persisted in the RunReport.evidence array, which flows through to frankensqlite via the existing storage pipeline. No new storage schema needed.

CAPACITY & BOUNDS:
- Default capacity: 500 entries (more than routing ledger's 200, because windows are smaller units)
- Old entries are evicted FIFO when capacity is reached
- total_recorded tracks the true count even after eviction

DEPENDS ON: scs.5 (CorrectionTracker for decisions), scs.7 (controller state for evidence entries)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:20:27.020961507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:19:08.507915211Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T23:19:08.507893440Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adaptive","evidence","phase-3","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-qlt.9","title":"Add TUI correction rendering with visual retraction markers","description":"PHASE 4 SURFACE INTEGRATION -- Makes corrections visible in the TUI.

FILE: src/tui.rs (extend existing, behind #[cfg(feature = "tui")])

WHY:
When the quality model corrects a fast model result, the TUI must show the user what happened. Without visual correction rendering, the user sees text appear, then silently change -- which is confusing and feels like a bug. With proper rendering, corrections are a feature: the user sees instant results with clear visual markers when corrections arrive.

CURRENT TUI STATE:
- LiveTranscriptionView (tui.rs:534-704): push_segment, scroll, auto_scroll
- SpeakerColorMap: ANSI color-coded speaker labels
- render_speaker_colored_line(): segment -> colored string
- SearchState, TranscriptFilter, TranscriptSearch: search/filter infrastructure

WHAT TO ADD:

1. CorrectionState tracking per-segment:
   pub(crate) enum SegmentCorrectionState {
       Original,       // Not part of any speculation (file mode, no --speculative)
       Speculative,    // Fast model result, pending quality confirmation
       Confirmed,      // Quality model agreed
       Retracted,      // Quality model disagreed, this segment is no longer valid
       Corrected,      // This segment is the quality model's replacement
   }

2. Extend LiveTranscriptionView:
   - Add correction_states: HashMap<usize, SegmentCorrectionState> (keyed by segment index)
   - Add push_speculative_segment(&mut self, segment, seq) -- marks as Speculative
   - Add retract_segment(&mut self, seq) -- finds segment by seq, marks Retracted
   - Add push_correction(&mut self, replaces_seq, corrected_segments) -- marks old as Retracted, new as Corrected
   - Add correction_count(&self) -> usize
   - Add speculation_stats_line(&self) -> String -- "Corrections: 3/47 windows (6.4%)"

3. Visual rendering rules:
   - Speculative segments: render with a subtle "~" prefix (e.g., "~ hello world")
   - Confirmed segments: render normally (remove "~" prefix)
   - Retracted segments: render with strikethrough ANSI codes (\x1b[9m...\x1b[0m) and dim color
   - Corrected segments: render with a brief highlight (bold for 2 ticks, then normal)
   - The correction transition should be visible but not jarring

4. Status bar extension:
   - Add speculation mode indicator: "[SPEC]" when speculative streaming is active
   - Show correction rate: "Corrections: 3/47 (6.4%)"
   - Show current window size: "Window: 3000ms"

5. Correction history panel (optional, toggled with 'c' key):
   - Shows last N corrections as a scrollable list
   - Each entry: "Window #47: 'hello wrold' -> 'hello world' (WER: 0.08)"
   - Useful for debugging and understanding pipeline behavior

DESIGN CHOICE -- WHY NOT JUST REPLACE TEXT SILENTLY:
- Users notice silent changes and find them unsettling
- Showing corrections builds trust ("the system is self-improving")
- The correction history is valuable for understanding model behavior
- Power users want to see correction rates to tune their model selection

BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY:
- When --speculative is not active, all segments are SegmentCorrectionState::Original
- No visual changes to non-speculative mode
- All existing TUI tests must continue to pass

DEPENDS ON: scs.5 (CorrectionTracker types for SegmentCorrectionState mapping)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:20:55.079345568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:13:30.244415940Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T23:13:30.244393508Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-4","rendering","streaming","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.9","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.9","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-qur","title":"rch: stabilize remote quality-gate execution for franken_whisper","description":"Remote quality gates are nondeterministic due to worker state drift and fail-open behavior: (1) some workers compile with stale /data/projects/asupersync causing E0308 in three_lane.rs; (2) some workers fail cargo test with no-space errors; (3) remote failures can trigger local fallback, violating offload policy. Investigate deterministic remote-only gate strategy for this repo and document/implement minimal local controls to keep cargo fmt/check/clippy/test reproducible via rch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:35:11.545757424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:53:09.834196150Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:53:09.834173227Z","close_reason":"Added rch quality-gate wrapper with remote-only enforcement, retry logic, worker block/restore control, and README runbook; validated fmt/check/clippy remote pass under controlled routing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-qur","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented repo-local remote quality-gate wrapper and documentation.

Changes:
- Added scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh:
  - Runs mandatory gates via rch (`fmt`, `check`, `clippy`, optional `test`).
  - Enforces remote-only semantics by failing on `[RCH] local` fallback.
  - Retries known transient/worker errors on fresh remote target dirs.
  - Added routing controls:
    - `BLOCK_WORKERS` (comma-separated) to temporarily disable problematic workers during a run.
    - Auto-restores disabled workers on script exit via trap.
  - Backward compatibility: `DRAIN_WORKERS` still accepted as alias input for `BLOCK_WORKERS`.
- Updated README Testing section with usage and env toggles:
  - `scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh`
  - `RUN_TEST_GATE=0`
  - `MAX_ATTEMPTS`
  - `BLOCK_WORKERS=vmi1149989`

Validation:
- Script syntax: `bash -n scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh` => pass.
- Lifecycle smoke: `MAX_ATTEMPTS=0 RUN_TEST_GATE=0 BLOCK_WORKERS=vmi1149989 scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh || true`
  - worker disable + auto-restore verified.
- Practical run: `MAX_ATTEMPTS=1 RUN_TEST_GATE=0 BLOCK_WORKERS=vmi1149989 scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh`

 {"id":"bd-20g","title":"Robot Mode: Agent-First NDJSON Interface Enhancement","description":"EPIC: Robot Mode Enhancement

Robot mode is the agent-first interface — line-oriented NDJSON events streamed in real-time. This is how other programs (AI agents, automation scripts, CI/CD) interact with franken_whisper. It MUST be:
- Stable schema (versioned, backward-compatible)
- Deterministic where possible
- Explicit error codes (enumerated, documented)
- Line-oriented (jsonl/ndjson)
- Easy to pipe into other tools

Current implementation is functional but can be enhanced:
1. Schema versioning: Include schema_version in every event so consumers can adapt
2. Error code enumeration: Replace ad-hoc error strings with structured codes
3. Progress estimation: Include estimated_progress_pct in stage events
4. Streaming partial transcripts: Emit partial transcript text as segments are produced (not just at completion)
5. Backend availability query: `robot backends` command to list available backends with capabilities
6. Run comparison: `robot diff <run1> <run2>` to compare transcription results
7. Health check: `robot health` to verify system readiness
8. Input validation: `robot validate --input <file>` to check input before committing to a run

The event schema should be documented as a JSON Schema artifact that can be consumed by typed language generators.

Key files: src/robot.rs (112 lines), src/main.rs (robot command handling)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Robot mode outputs stable, versioned NDJSON schema
- All events have explicit error codes
- Schema versioning supports backward compatibility
- Event ordering is guaranteed monotonic
- All robot mode contract tests pass.","acceptance_criteria":"Robot mode outputs stable, versioned NDJSON schema. All events have explicit error codes. Schema versioning supports backward compatibility. Event ordering is guaranteed monotonic. All robot mode contract tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:08:47.305701227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T20:46:02.725333019Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T20:46:02.725313763Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-ergonomics","epic","phase-2","robot-mode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20g","depends_on_id":"bd-1rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20g.1","title":"Implement robot event schema versioning","description":"Robot mode NDJSON output must be stable. Add schema versioning:

1. Every event includes schema_version: "1.0"
2. robot schema command emits full JSON Schema document
3. Version bump rules: Minor for new optional fields, Major for breaking changes
4. Schema as cargo-embedded resource

Events to version: run_start, stage, run_complete, run_error, run_cancelled (new), partial_transcript (new), backend_probe (new).

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_schema_version_present: Every emit_robot_* function includes schema_version field
- test_schema_valid_json_schema: robot schema output is valid JSON Schema (parse with serde)
- test_all_events_match_schema: Generate each event type, validate against schema
- test_schema_backward_compat: New schema accepts old events (additive only)
- test_schema_command_output: cargo run -- robot schema → valid JSON on stdout
- test_schema_embedded_resource: Schema accessible at runtime without filesystem

LOGGING:
- "Emitting robot event: {event_type}, schema_version={ver}" at TRACE

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every robot event includes a schema_version field
- Version follows semver (major.minor)
- Breaking changes increment major version
- Consumers can check version before parsing
- Unit test: all event types include schema_version
- Unit test: version string parses as valid semver.","acceptance_criteria":"Every robot event includes a schema_version field. Version follows semver (major.minor).  {"id":"bd-3i1.3","title":"Add diagnostic PRAGMAs for storage observability","description":"frankensqlite supports diagnostic PRAGMAs that expose internal state. Add observability endpoints:

3. Page cache: Hit/miss ratios, dirty page count
4. MVCC state: Active snapshots, version chain depth
5. Storage size: DB file size, WAL size, total pages

Expose these via:
- Robot mode: `robot storage-stats` command emitting NDJSON
- TUI: Status line showing active transactions, DB size
- CLI: `storage-info` command for debugging

 {"id":"bd-3i1.4","title":"Implement schema versioning with migration support","description":"Schema evolution for the three-table DB (runs, segments, events).

Migration framework:
1. _meta table: key=schema_version, value=N
2. On open: check version, run forward migrations
3. Each migration in a transaction
4. Never drop columns (only add)

Planned migrations: v2 (acceleration_json), v3 (speaker_embeddings), v4 (vad_segments), v5 (aligned_words).

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_fresh_db_at_latest_version: New DB has current schema version
- test_migration_v1_to_v2: Open v1 DB → migrate → verify new columns exist
- test_migration_idempotent: Run migration twice → no error
- test_migration_preserves_data: Populate v1 DB → migrate → all data intact
- test_migration_transactional: Inject error mid-migration → ROLLBACK, DB unchanged
- test_version_mismatch_detection: Open DB with future version → clear error
- test_meta_table_created: _meta table exists with correct schema_version
- test_jsonl_includes_version: JSONL export manifest includes schema version

LOGGING:
- "DB schema version: current={cur}, expected={exp}" at INFO on open
- "Migrating schema: v{from} → v{to}" at INFO per migration
- "Migration v{N} complete: {changes_summary}" at INFO

## Acceptance Criteria
- RaptorQ encoding applied to critical DB pages
- Recovery from simulated corruption restores data without loss
- Durability test: corrupt N random bytes, verify recovery succeeds
- Performance: encoding overhead under 5% for normal operations
- Fallback: graceful degradation if RaptorQ unavailable.","acceptance_criteria":"- RaptorQ encoding applied to critical DB pages. - Recovery from simulated corruption restores data without loss. - Durability test: corrupt N random bytes, verify recovery succeeds. - Performance: encoding overhead under 5% for normal operations. - Fallback: graceful degradation if RaptorQ unavailable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:13:36.835763506Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T19:22:26.214967861Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T19:22:26.214926404Z","close_reason":"Schema versioning with _meta table, SCHEMA_VERSION=2, forward-only migrations, DELETE+INSERT for fsqlite INSERT OR REPLACE bug. 6 tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensqlite","migration","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3i1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3i1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3i1.5","title":"Add index optimization for common query patterns","description":"Optimize query performance with targeted indexes.

Current queries:
- list_recent_runs: ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT n → needs INDEX on runs(started_at)
- load_run_details: WHERE id = ? → already has PRIMARY KEY
- segments by run: WHERE run_id = ? ORDER BY idx → needs INDEX on segments(run_id, idx) (already PRIMARY KEY)
- events by run: WHERE run_id = ? ORDER BY seq → needs INDEX on events(run_id, seq) (already PRIMARY KEY)

New indexes needed for planned features:
- Search transcript: CREATE INDEX idx_runs_transcript ON runs(transcript) — but TEXT search needs FTS
- Filter by backend: CREATE INDEX idx_runs_backend ON runs(backend)
- Filter by time range: Already covered by started_at index
- Speaker search in segments: CREATE INDEX idx_segments_speaker ON segments(speaker)
- Confidence filtering: CREATE INDEX idx_segments_confidence ON segments(confidence)

Consider frankensqlite's FTS5 extension for full-text search over transcripts.

Performance targets:
- list_recent_runs(100): < 1ms
- load_run_details: < 5ms (even with 1000+ segments)
- transcript search: < 50ms (with FTS5)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Indexes exist on: runs(created_at), runs(backend), segments(run_id), events(run_id, seq)
- Query performance: list_runs with limit 100 under 10ms on 10K rows
- Query performance: get_segments_for_run under 5ms
- Index creation is idempotent (safe to re-run)
- Unit test: query plan shows index usage (EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN).","acceptance_criteria":"- Indexes exist on: runs(created_at), runs(backend), segments(run_id), events(run_id, seq). - Query performance: list_runs with limit 100 under 10ms on 10K rows. - Query performance: get_segments_for_run under 5ms. - Index creation is idempotent (safe to re-run). - Unit test: query plan shows index usage (EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:13:36.917328269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T21:19:59.907615781Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T21:19:59.907595493Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensqlite","indexes","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3i1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3i1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3i1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3i1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3pf","title":"Testing Infrastructure: Comprehensive Quality Gates","description":"EPIC: Testing Infrastructure

Testing is a first-class concern. The AGENTS.md mandates:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test

Each module needs:
- Unit tests: happy path, edge cases, error handling
- Integration tests: file-based transcription, robot mode JSON contracts, SQLite+JSONL sync/recovery, ffmpeg behavior
- E2E tests: full pipeline from audio file to persisted result

Special testing requirements:
1. Cancellation testing: Use asupersync lab runtime to inject cancellation at every pipeline stage, verify no resource leaks
2. Robot mode contract tests: Validate NDJSON output against JSON Schema
3. Backend mock tests: Test pipeline without real backend binaries (mock subprocess output)
4. TUI snapshot tests: Render TUI to buffer, compare against golden snapshots
5. Performance benchmarks: Track regression in processing time, memory usage
6. Sync roundtrip tests: Export → import → compare, verify no data loss
7. Concurrent persistence tests: Multiple runs writing simultaneously via MVCC
8. TTY audio codec roundtrip: Encode → decode → compare, verify lossless (within codec tolerance)

Testing tools: cargo test, criterion (benchmarks), proptest (property testing), insta (snapshot testing)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test infrastructure supports: mock backends, golden file comparison, E2E pipeline tests, structured logging verification
- All modules have comprehensive unit tests
- E2E test harness exercises full transcription flow
- Performance benchmarks establish baselines
- All tests pass in CI (no external dependencies required).","acceptance_criteria":"Test infrastructure supports: mock backends, golden file comparison, E2E pipeline tests, structured logging verification. All modules have comprehensive unit tests. E2E test harness exercises full transcription flow. Performance benchmarks establish baselines. All tests pass in CI (no external dependencies required).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:08:47.627035239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T22:06:51.405588838Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T22:06:51.405569281Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","phase-0","quality","testing"]}

 {"id":"bd-efr.4","title":"Implement evidence ledger system for all adaptive decisions","description":"ALIEN-ARTIFACT: Evidence Ledger

Every adaptive decision produces an evidence ledger artifact for auditability.

Schema: controller, decision_id (UUID), timestamp (RFC3339), state (JSON), actions_considered (vec of {action, expected_loss, confidence}), action_selected, posterior, loss_estimate, calibration_metric, fallback_triggered, fallback_reason, outcome (post-hoc).

Storage: events table stage=evidence_ledger, also in JSONL export.
Query: robot evidence --run <id>, CLI evidence --last 10, TUI evidence pane.

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_ledger_schema_complete: Every ledger has ALL required fields populated
- test_ledger_uuid_unique: Each decision gets a unique ID
- test_ledger_timestamps_valid: All timestamps are valid RFC3339
- test_ledger_round_trip: Create → persist → load → compare (no data loss)
- test_ledger_query_by_run: Load all ledgers for a specific run_id
- test_ledger_query_recent: Load last N ledgers across all runs
- test_ledger_in_jsonl_export: Evidence ledgers included in JSONL export
- test_outcome_backfill: After execution, outcome field is populated

LOGGING:
- "Evidence ledger created: controller={ctrl}, decision={id}" at DEBUG
- "Evidence ledger outcome: {id} → {outcome_summary}" at DEBUG

## Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence ledger persists: every routing decision, shadow mode results, calibration updates, backend performance metrics
- Ledger stored in frankensqlite alongside run data
- Ledger queryable for trend analysis (which backend is improving/degrading)
- Unit test: routing decision persisted and retrievable
- Unit test: ledger grows monotonically (append-only)
- Export: ledger included in JSONL sync exports.","acceptance_criteria":"Evidence ledger persists: every routing decision, shadow mode results, calibration updates, backend performance metrics. Ledger stored in frankensqlite alongside run data. Ledger queryable for trend analysis (which backend is improving/degrading). Unit test: routing decision persisted and retrievable. Unit test: ledger grows monotonically (append-only).  {"id":"bd-qla","title":"Pipeline Enhancement: Advanced Multi-Stage ASR Processing","description":"EPIC: Pipeline Enhancement

The current pipeline has 5 stages: ingest → normalize → backend → accelerate → persist. This is a good start but misses several powerful capabilities from the legacy systems.

New stages to add (inspired by whisper-diarization's multi-stage approach):
1. VAD Pre-filtering: Voice Activity Detection to identify speech segments before sending to ASR. Dramatically reduces processing time for audio with lots of silence/noise. Inspired by whisper.cpp's Silero-VAD integration.
2. Source Separation: Isolate vocals from background noise/music (Demucs-inspired). Improves speaker embedding accuracy and ASR quality on noisy audio.
3. Forced Alignment: CTC-based timestamp correction after initial ASR. Whisper's timestamps can drift at segment boundaries; forced alignment uses emission probabilities to find true boundaries. Critical for accurate word-level speaker mapping.
4. Speaker Diarization: TitaNet-inspired speaker embedding + clustering. Identify who said what. Currently delegated entirely to backends; bringing this in-house gives more control.
5. Punctuation Restoration: Multilingual punctuation model (12+ languages). The diarization pipeline strips punctuation during alignment; restoration makes transcripts readable.

Pipeline should also support:
- Skip/include stages based on request (e.g., skip diarization if not needed)
- Stage-level caching (don't re-normalize if already WAV)
- Parallel stage execution where possible (e.g., VAD + source separation)
- Progress reporting per stage (for robot mode and TUI)

Each new stage must follow the existing EventLog pattern — emit stage start/complete events with payloads for observability.

Key architectural decision: New stages should be composable and optional, not monolithic. The pipeline configuration should be declarative.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pipeline includes all stages: ingest, normalize, VAD, source separation, backend, alignment, diarization, punctuation, accelerate, persist
- Stages are composable and independently testable
- Each stage emits robot events
- Pipeline handles errors at any stage with proper cleanup
- All pipeline tests pass.","acceptance_criteria":"Pipeline includes all stages: ingest, normalize, VAD, source separation, backend, alignment, diarization, punctuation, accelerate, persist. Stages are composable and independently testable. Each stage emits robot events. Pipeline handles errors at any stage with proper cleanup. All pipeline tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:08:47.223292539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T21:24:27.767745727Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T21:24:27.767721011Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","phase-3","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qla","depends_on_id":"bd-1rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qla","depends_on_id":"bd-38c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-qlt.8","title":"Implement CorrectionEvidenceLedger with per-window correction tracking","description":"PHASE 3 ADAPTIVE INTELLIGENCE -- Persistent evidence ledger that records every speculation decision for analysis, debugging, and adaptive improvement.

FILE: src/speculation.rs (extend)

WHY:
The existing RoutingEvidenceLedger (backend/mod.rs) tracks backend selection decisions. The CorrectionEvidenceLedger is its speculation analog: it records every window's fast/quality comparison, correction decision, and drift metrics. This serves three purposes:
1. Post-hoc analysis: "Why did the pipeline correct window 47?"
2. Adaptive control: The SpeculationWindowController reads this to tune window size
3. Debugging: Full replay of every decision for deterministic reproduction

RELATIONSHIP TO EXISTING EVIDENCE INFRASTRUCTURE:
- RoutingEvidenceLedger (backend/mod.rs:229-381) -- tracks backend selection, capacity 200
- PipelineCx::record_evidence() (orchestrator.rs) -- accepts Value entries
- EvidenceLedgerBuilder (accelerate.rs) -- builds acceleration evidence
- This new CorrectionEvidenceLedger follows the same pattern: structured entries, bounded capacity, JSON export

TYPES:

pub struct CorrectionEvidenceLedger {
    entries: VecDeque<CorrectionEvidenceEntry>,
    capacity: usize,
    total_recorded: u64,
}

pub struct CorrectionEvidenceEntry {
    pub entry_id: u64,
    pub window_id: u64,
    pub run_id: String,
    pub timestamp_rfc3339: String,
    
    // Fast model info
    pub fast_model_id: String,
    pub fast_latency_ms: u64,
    pub fast_confidence_mean: f64,
    pub fast_segment_count: usize,
    
    // Quality model info
    pub quality_model_id: String,
    pub quality_latency_ms: u64,
    pub quality_confidence_mean: f64,
    pub quality_segment_count: usize,
    
    // Drift metrics
    pub drift: CorrectionDrift,
    
    // Decision
    pub decision: String,          // "confirmed" or "corrected"
    pub tolerance_snapshot: CorrectionTolerance,
    
    // Controller state at decision time
    pub window_size_ms: u64,
    pub correction_rate_at_decision: f64,
    pub controller_action: String, // "shrink", "hold", "grow"
    pub controller_confidence: f64,
    pub fallback_active: bool,
    pub fallback_reason: Option<String>,
}

impl CorrectionEvidenceLedger {
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self
    pub fn record(&mut self, entry: CorrectionEvidenceEntry)
    pub fn total_recorded(&self) -> u64
    pub fn entries(&self) -> &VecDeque<CorrectionEvidenceEntry>
    pub fn to_evidence_json(&self) -> Value  // For PipelineCx::record_evidence()
    pub fn diagnostics(&self) -> Value       // Summary stats as JSON
    
    // Analysis methods
    pub fn correction_rate(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_fast_latency(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_quality_latency(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_wer(&self) -> f64
    pub fn latency_savings_pct(&self) -> f64  // How much faster user sees results vs quality-only
    
    // Window size trend
    pub fn window_size_trend(&self) -> Vec<(u64, u64)>  // (window_id, window_size_ms) pairs
}

INTEGRATION WITH PipelineCx:
At the end of a speculative run, the pipeline calls:
  pcx.record_evidence(&correction_ledger.to_evidence_json());
This makes the correction evidence part of the run's permanent evidence trail, alongside routing evidence and acceleration evidence.

INTEGRATION WITH STORAGE:
The evidence entries should be persisted in the RunReport.evidence array, which flows through to frankensqlite via the existing storage pipeline. No new storage schema needed.

CAPACITY & BOUNDS:
- Default capacity: 500 entries (more than routing ledger's 200, because windows are smaller units)
- Old entries are evicted FIFO when capacity is reached
- total_recorded tracks the true count even after eviction

DEPENDS ON: scs.5 (CorrectionTracker for decisions), scs.7 (controller state for evidence entries)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:20:27.020961507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:19:08.507915211Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T23:19:08.507893440Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adaptive","evidence","phase-3","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.8","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-qlt.9","title":"Add TUI correction rendering with visual retraction markers","description":"PHASE 4 SURFACE INTEGRATION -- Makes corrections visible in the TUI.

FILE: src/tui.rs (extend existing, behind #[cfg(feature = "tui")])

WHY:
When the quality model corrects a fast model result, the TUI must show the user what happened. Without visual correction rendering, the user sees text appear, then silently change -- which is confusing and feels like a bug. With proper rendering, corrections are a feature: the user sees instant results with clear visual markers when corrections arrive.

CURRENT TUI STATE:
- LiveTranscriptionView (tui.rs:534-704): push_segment, scroll, auto_scroll
- SpeakerColorMap: ANSI color-coded speaker labels
- render_speaker_colored_line(): segment -> colored string
- SearchState, TranscriptFilter, TranscriptSearch: search/filter infrastructure

WHAT TO ADD:

1. CorrectionState tracking per-segment:
   pub(crate) enum SegmentCorrectionState {
       Original,       // Not part of any speculation (file mode, no --speculative)
       Speculative,    // Fast model result, pending quality confirmation
       Confirmed,      // Quality model agreed
       Retracted,      // Quality model disagreed, this segment is no longer valid
       Corrected,      // This segment is the quality model's replacement
   }

2. Extend LiveTranscriptionView:
   - Add correction_states: HashMap<usize, SegmentCorrectionState> (keyed by segment index)
   - Add push_speculative_segment(&mut self, segment, seq) -- marks as Speculative
   - Add retract_segment(&mut self, seq) -- finds segment by seq, marks Retracted
   - Add push_correction(&mut self, replaces_seq, corrected_segments) -- marks old as Retracted, new as Corrected
   - Add correction_count(&self) -> usize
   - Add speculation_stats_line(&self) -> String -- "Corrections: 3/47 windows (6.4%)"

3. Visual rendering rules:
   - Speculative segments: render with a subtle "~" prefix (e.g., "~ hello world")
   - Confirmed segments: render normally (remove "~" prefix)
   - Retracted segments: render with strikethrough ANSI codes (\x1b[9m...\x1b[0m) and dim color
   - Corrected segments: render with a brief highlight (bold for 2 ticks, then normal)
   - The correction transition should be visible but not jarring

4. Status bar extension:
   - Add speculation mode indicator: "[SPEC]" when speculative streaming is active
   - Show correction rate: "Corrections: 3/47 (6.4%)"
   - Show current window size: "Window: 3000ms"

5. Correction history panel (optional, toggled with 'c' key):
   - Shows last N corrections as a scrollable list
   - Each entry: "Window #47: 'hello wrold' -> 'hello world' (WER: 0.08)"
   - Useful for debugging and understanding pipeline behavior

DESIGN CHOICE -- WHY NOT JUST REPLACE TEXT SILENTLY:
- Users notice silent changes and find them unsettling
- Showing corrections builds trust ("the system is self-improving")
- The correction history is valuable for understanding model behavior
- Power users want to see correction rates to tune their model selection

BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY:
- When --speculative is not active, all segments are SegmentCorrectionState::Original
- No visual changes to non-speculative mode
- All existing TUI tests must continue to pass

DEPENDS ON: scs.5 (CorrectionTracker types for SegmentCorrectionState mapping)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:20:55.079345568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:13:30.244415940Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T23:13:30.244393508Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-4","rendering","streaming","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.9","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qlt.9","depends_on_id":"bd-qlt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-qur","title":"rch: stabilize remote quality-gate execution for franken_whisper","description":"Remote quality gates are nondeterministic due to worker state drift and fail-open behavior: (1) some workers compile with stale /data/projects/asupersync causing E0308 in three_lane.rs; (2) some workers fail cargo test with no-space errors; (3) remote failures can trigger local fallback, violating offload policy. Investigate deterministic remote-only gate strategy for this repo and document/implement minimal local controls to keep cargo fmt/check/clippy/test reproducible via rch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:35:11.545757424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:53:09.834196150Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:53:09.834173227Z","close_reason":"Added rch quality-gate wrapper with remote-only enforcement, retry logic, worker block/restore control, and README runbook; validated fmt/check/clippy remote pass under controlled routing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-qur","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented repo-local remote quality-gate wrapper and documentation.

Changes:
- Added scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh:
  - Runs mandatory gates via rch (`fmt`, `check`, `clippy`, optional `test`).
  - Enforces remote-only semantics by failing on `[RCH] local` fallback.
  - Retries known transient/worker errors on fresh remote target dirs.
  - Added routing controls:
    - `BLOCK_WORKERS` (comma-separated) to temporarily disable problematic workers during a run.
    - Auto-restores disabled workers on script exit via trap.
  - Backward compatibility: `DRAIN_WORKERS` still accepted as alias input for `BLOCK_WORKERS`.
- Updated README Testing section with usage and env toggles:
  - `scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh`
  - `RUN_TEST_GATE=0`
  - `MAX_ATTEMPTS`
  - `BLOCK_WORKERS=vmi1149989`

Validation:
- Script syntax: `bash -n scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh` => pass.
- Lifecycle smoke: `MAX_ATTEMPTS=0 RUN_TEST_GATE=0 BLOCK_WORKERS=vmi1149989 scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh || true`
  - worker disable + auto-restore verified.
- Practical run: `MAX_ATTEMPTS=1 RUN_TEST_GATE=0 BLOCK_WORKERS=vmi1149989 scripts/run_quality_gates_rch.sh`
  - pass: fmt/check/clippy all succeeded remotely.
  - scheduler did not route to blocked worker.

Residual risk:
- Full `cargo test` remains the longest/highest-pressure gate and can still fail on worker storage/toolchain instability; wrapper now provides deterministic controls and explicit fail surfaces rather than silent/local fallback.

 {"id":"bd-20g","title":"Robot Mode: Agent-First NDJSON Interface Enhancement","description":"EPIC: Robot Mode Enhancement

Robot mode is the agent-first interface — line-oriented NDJSON events streamed in real-time. This is how other programs (AI agents, automation scripts, CI/CD) interact with franken_whisper. It MUST be:
- Stable schema (versioned, backward-compatible)
- Deterministic where possible
- Explicit error codes (enumerated, documented)
- Line-oriented (jsonl/ndjson)
- Easy to pipe into other tools

Current implementation is functional but can be enhanced:
1. Schema versioning: Include schema_version in every event so consumers can adapt
2. Error code enumeration: Replace ad-hoc error strings with structured codes
3. Progress estimation: Include estimated_progress_pct in stage events
4. Streaming partial transcripts: Emit partial transcript text as segments are produced (not just at completion)
5. Backend availability query: `robot backends` command to list available backends with capabilities
6. Run comparison: `robot diff <run1> <run2>` to compare transcription results
7. Health check: `robot health` to verify system readiness
8. Input validation: `robot validate --input <file>` to check input before committing to a run

The event schema should be documented as a JSON Schema artifact that can be consumed by typed language generators.

Key files: src/robot.rs (112 lines), src/main.rs (robot command handling)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Robot mode outputs stable, versioned NDJSON schema
- All events have explicit error codes
- Schema versioning supports backward compatibility
- Event ordering is guaranteed monotonic
- All robot mode contract tests pass.","acceptance_criteria":"Robot mode outputs stable, versioned NDJSON schema. All events have explicit error codes. Schema versioning supports backward compatibility. Event ordering is guaranteed monotonic. All robot mode contract tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:08:47.305701227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T20:46:02.725333019Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T20:46:02.725313763Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-ergonomics","epic","phase-2","robot-mode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20g","depends_on_id":"bd-1rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-20g.1","title":"Implement robot event schema versioning","description":"Robot mode NDJSON output must be stable. Add schema versioning:

1. Every event includes schema_version: "1.0"
2. robot schema command emits full JSON Schema document
3. Version bump rules: Minor for new optional fields, Major for breaking changes
4. Schema as cargo-embedded resource

Events to version: run_start, stage, run_complete, run_error, run_cancelled (new), partial_transcript (new), backend_probe (new).

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_schema_version_present: Every emit_robot_* function includes schema_version field
- test_schema_valid_json_schema: robot schema output is valid JSON Schema (parse with serde)
- test_all_events_match_schema: Generate each event type, validate against schema
- test_schema_backward_compat: New schema accepts old events (additive only)
- test_schema_command_output: cargo run -- robot schema → valid JSON on stdout
- test_schema_embedded_resource: Schema accessible at runtime without filesystem

LOGGING:
- "Emitting robot event: {event_type}, schema_version={ver}" at TRACE

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every robot event includes a schema_version field
- Version follows semver (major.minor)
- Breaking changes increment major version
- Consumers can check version before parsing
- Unit test: all event types include schema_version
- Unit test: version string parses as valid semver.","acceptance_criteria":"Every robot event includes a schema_version field. Version follows semver (major.minor).  {"id":"bd-3i1.3","title":"Add diagnostic PRAGMAs for storage observability","description":"frankensqlite supports diagnostic PRAGMAs that expose internal state. Add observability endpoints:

1. Transaction stats: Active transactions, commits/rollbacks per second

3. Page cache: Hit/miss ratios, dirty page count
4. MVCC state: Active snapshots, version chain depth
5. Storage size: DB file size, WAL size, total pages

Expose these via:
- Robot mode: `robot storage-stats` command emitting NDJSON
- TUI: Status line showing active transactions, DB size
- CLI: `storage-info` command for debugging

 {"id":"bd-3i1.4","title":"Implement schema versioning with migration support","description":"Schema evolution for the three-table DB (runs, segments, events).

Migration framework:
1. _meta table: key=schema_version, value=N
2. On open: check version, run forward migrations
3. Each migration in a transaction
4. Never drop columns (only add)

Planned migrations: v2 (acceleration_json), v3 (speaker_embeddings), v4 (vad_segments), v5 (aligned_words).

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_fresh_db_at_latest_version: New DB has current schema version
- test_migration_v1_to_v2: Open v1 DB → migrate → verify new columns exist
- test_migration_idempotent: Run migration twice → no error
- test_migration_preserves_data: Populate v1 DB → migrate → all data intact
- test_migration_transactional: Inject error mid-migration → ROLLBACK, DB unchanged
- test_version_mismatch_detection: Open DB with future version → clear error
- test_meta_table_created: _meta table exists with correct schema_version
- test_jsonl_includes_version: JSONL export manifest includes schema version

LOGGING:
- "DB schema version: current={cur}, expected={exp}" at INFO on open
- "Migrating schema: v{from} → v{to}" at INFO per migration
- "Migration v{N} complete: {changes_summary}" at INFO

## Acceptance Criteria
- RaptorQ encoding applied to critical DB pages
- Recovery from simulated corruption restores data without loss
- Durability test: corrupt N random bytes, verify recovery succeeds
- Performance: encoding overhead under 5% for normal operations
- Fallback: graceful degradation if RaptorQ unavailable.","acceptance_criteria":"- RaptorQ encoding applied to critical DB pages. - Recovery from simulated corruption restores data without loss. - Durability test: corrupt N random bytes, verify recovery succeeds. - Performance: encoding overhead under 5% for normal operations. - Fallback: graceful degradation if RaptorQ unavailable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:13:36.835763506Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T19:22:26.214967861Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T19:22:26.214926404Z","close_reason":"Schema versioning with _meta table, SCHEMA_VERSION=2, forward-only migrations, DELETE+INSERT for fsqlite INSERT OR REPLACE bug. 6 tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensqlite","migration","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3i1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3i1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3i1.5","title":"Add index optimization for common query patterns","description":"Optimize query performance with targeted indexes.

Current queries:
- list_recent_runs: ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT n → needs INDEX on runs(started_at)
- load_run_details: WHERE id = ? → already has PRIMARY KEY
- segments by run: WHERE run_id = ? ORDER BY idx → needs INDEX on segments(run_id, idx) (already PRIMARY KEY)
- events by run: WHERE run_id = ? ORDER BY seq → needs INDEX on events(run_id, seq) (already PRIMARY KEY)

New indexes needed for planned features:
- Search transcript: CREATE INDEX idx_runs_transcript ON runs(transcript) — but TEXT search needs FTS
- Filter by backend: CREATE INDEX idx_runs_backend ON runs(backend)
- Filter by time range: Already covered by started_at index
- Speaker search in segments: CREATE INDEX idx_segments_speaker ON segments(speaker)
- Confidence filtering: CREATE INDEX idx_segments_confidence ON segments(confidence)

Consider frankensqlite's FTS5 extension for full-text search over transcripts.

Performance targets:
- list_recent_runs(100): < 1ms
- load_run_details: < 5ms (even with 1000+ segments)
- transcript search: < 50ms (with FTS5)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Indexes exist on: runs(created_at), runs(backend), segments(run_id), events(run_id, seq)
- Query performance: list_runs with limit 100 under 10ms on 10K rows
- Query performance: get_segments_for_run under 5ms
- Index creation is idempotent (safe to re-run)
- Unit test: query plan shows index usage (EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN).","acceptance_criteria":"- Indexes exist on: runs(created_at), runs(backend), segments(run_id), events(run_id, seq). - Query performance: list_runs with limit 100 under 10ms on 10K rows. - Query performance: get_segments_for_run under 5ms. - Index creation is idempotent (safe to re-run). - Unit test: query plan shows index usage (EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:13:36.917328269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T21:19:59.907615781Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T21:19:59.907595493Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensqlite","indexes","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3i1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3i1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3i1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3i1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3pf","title":"Testing Infrastructure: Comprehensive Quality Gates","description":"EPIC: Testing Infrastructure

Testing is a first-class concern. The AGENTS.md mandates:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test

Each module needs:
- Unit tests: happy path, edge cases, error handling
- Integration tests: file-based transcription, robot mode JSON contracts, SQLite+JSONL sync/recovery, ffmpeg behavior
- E2E tests: full pipeline from audio file to persisted result

Special testing requirements:
1. Cancellation testing: Use asupersync lab runtime to inject cancellation at every pipeline stage, verify no resource leaks
2. Robot mode contract tests: Validate NDJSON output against JSON Schema
3. Backend mock tests: Test pipeline without real backend binaries (mock subprocess output)
4. TUI snapshot tests: Render TUI to buffer, compare against golden snapshots
5. Performance benchmarks: Track regression in processing time, memory usage
6. Sync roundtrip tests: Export → import → compare, verify no data loss
7. Concurrent persistence tests: Multiple runs writing simultaneously via MVCC
8. TTY audio codec roundtrip: Encode → decode → compare, verify lossless (within codec tolerance)

Testing tools: cargo test, criterion (benchmarks), proptest (property testing), insta (snapshot testing)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test infrastructure supports: mock backends, golden file comparison, E2E pipeline tests, structured logging verification
- All modules have comprehensive unit tests
- E2E test harness exercises full transcription flow
- Performance benchmarks establish baselines
- All tests pass in CI (no external dependencies required).","acceptance_criteria":"Test infrastructure supports: mock backends, golden file comparison, E2E pipeline tests, structured logging verification. All modules have comprehensive unit tests. E2E test harness exercises full transcription flow. Performance benchmarks establish baselines. All tests pass in CI (no external dependencies required).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:08:47.627035239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T22:06:51.405588838Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T22:06:51.405569281Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","phase-0","quality","testing"]}

 {"id":"bd-efr.4","title":"Implement evidence ledger system for all adaptive decisions","description":"ALIEN-ARTIFACT: Evidence Ledger

Every adaptive decision produces an evidence ledger artifact for auditability.

Schema: controller, decision_id (UUID), timestamp (RFC3339), state (JSON), actions_considered (vec of {action, expected_loss, confidence}), action_selected, posterior, loss_estimate, calibration_metric, fallback_triggered, fallback_reason, outcome (post-hoc).

Storage: events table stage=evidence_ledger, also in JSONL export.
Query: robot evidence --run <id>, CLI evidence --last 10, TUI evidence pane.

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_ledger_schema_complete: Every ledger has ALL required fields populated
- test_ledger_uuid_unique: Each decision gets a unique ID
- test_ledger_timestamps_valid: All timestamps are valid RFC3339
- test_ledger_round_trip: Create → persist → load → compare (no data loss)
- test_ledger_query_by_run: Load all ledgers for a specific run_id
- test_ledger_query_recent: Load last N ledgers across all runs
- test_ledger_in_jsonl_export: Evidence ledgers included in JSONL export
- test_outcome_backfill: After execution, outcome field is populated

LOGGING:
- "Evidence ledger created: controller={ctrl}, decision={id}" at DEBUG
- "Evidence ledger outcome: {id} → {outcome_summary}" at DEBUG

## Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence ledger persists: every routing decision, shadow mode results, calibration updates, backend performance metrics
- Ledger stored in frankensqlite alongside run data
- Ledger queryable for trend analysis (which backend is improving/degrading)
- Unit test: routing decision persisted and retrievable
- Unit test: ledger grows monotonically (append-only)
- Export: ledger included in JSONL sync exports.","acceptance_criteria":"Evidence ledger persists: every routing decision, shadow mode results, calibration updates, backend performance metrics.
